meta.name: 10k Performance
meta.subtitle: Break 40 minutes — elite club target
meta.description: A 20-week advanced programme for competitive runners targeting sub-40 minutes for 10k. Built around VO2max intervals, lactate threshold runs, and aerobic volume, with two gym sessions per week for strength and injury prevention.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-40 for 10k requires 4:00/km for 10km. You need both a very high VO2max and exceptional lactate threshold. This plan attacks both. The interval sessions build the ceiling; the threshold runs raise the floor. Do not miss the threshold sessions — they are as important as the intervals.

weeks.title: Base establishment
weeks.note: Opening week for a serious 10k programme. Five running days, two quality sessions. Nothing maximal — establish your baseline and get the body used to the structure.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 6x800m @ current 10k pace (2min rec) · 2km CD · 11km total

weeks.title: VO2max introduction
weeks.note: First VO2max session. The 1km reps at 3:45–3:55/km are harder than 10k pace — they develop the aerobic ceiling that makes 4:00/km feel sustainable.

weeks.title: Volume build
weeks.note: Volume peaks in the first block. The long run reaches 16km. Keep the long run genuinely easy — this is aerobic base work, not race preparation.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Deliberate recovery week. Volume drops by 35–40%. The fitness from the previous block consolidates now. Rest is the training instruction this week.

weeks.title: Race pace introduction
weeks.note: Phase 2 of the base block introduces race-pace running. The 1200m reps at exactly 4:00/km build the specific endurance for your goal.

weeks.title: Mixed quality
weeks.note: Mixed speed session. The combination of 800m and 400m reps in the same session trains your body to handle pace changes — essential for racing.

weeks.title: Volume peak 2
weeks.note: Second volume peak before the halfway deload. The long run has a race-pace section built in — it trains your body to run at goal pace under fatigue.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Deliberate recovery week. Volume drops by 35–40%. The fitness from the previous block consolidates now. Rest is the training instruction this week.

weeks.title: Speed sharpening
weeks.note: Phase 2 opens with pure speed work. The 300m reps at mile pace train fast-twitch recruitment and make 4:00/km feel slow by comparison.

weeks.title: Race simulation
weeks.note: The 8km time trial on Tuesday is the most important session of the plan. Run it at goal race pace — 4:00/km — and note exactly how it feels. This tells you whether your goal is realistic with 10 weeks remaining.

weeks.title: Peak block 1
weeks.note: First peak week. The 4x1600m session is the cornerstone of the plan — 4 reps of 1600m at race pace develops the specific endurance to hold 4:00/km for 10km.

weeks.title: Peak block 2
weeks.note: Second peak week. Same sessions as last week — the repetition is intentional. Consistency builds fitness; variation breaks it.

weeks.title: Peak block 3
weeks.note: Third and final peak week. Race-pace 1km reps — confident, controlled, owning the pace. Three weeks until race day.

weeks.title: Taper — week 1
weeks.note: Taper begins. Volume drops, intensity stays. Your legs will feel sluggish then suddenly light — trust this process completely.

sessions.det: 1.5km WU · 6x600m @ race pace (2min rec) · 1.5km CD · 7.5km total

weeks.title: Taper — week 2
weeks.note: Second taper week. By Thursday you should feel genuinely good. One week to race day — stay calm and trust your training.

sessions.det: 1.5km WU · 4x600m @ race pace (2min rec) · 1.5km CD · 6km total

weeks.title: Race week
weeks.note: Race week. Rest. Eat well. Sleep well. Everything this week serves Saturday's race. The fitness is built.

sessions.det: 2km easy WU · 4x100m strides · Race: through 1km at 4:02, settle to 4:00, build from 7km · Negative split target

nut.d: Porridge with banana or toast with peanut butter. 500ml water. Only familiar foods.
200ml water. Warm up 15 minutes easy. Include strides if planned.
Take water at stations. For half marathon and above: gel at 6–7km and again at 11–12km if trained with gels.
Water immediately. 400–500ml in 10 minutes. Keep moving to cool down gently.
Full meal within 90 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, everything. You have done something genuinely impressive.

weeks.title: Recovery
weeks.note: Race week overflow — if race is on Sunday of week 16, use this week for gentle recovery.

weeks.title: Training week 17
weeks.note: Week 17 — progressive training continues.

weeks.title: Training week 18
weeks.note: Week 18 — progressive training continues.

weeks.title: Training week 19
weeks.note: Week 19 — progressive training continues.
